text:
Diehard Australian basketball fans have been left devastated after the Boomers blew a 24-point lead against Serbia, knocking the Aussies out of the 2024 Paris Olympic Games.

Diehard fans have been devastated after the Australian men’s basketball team, the Boomers, crashed out of the Olympics after blowing its 24-point lead over Serbia.

The Aussies were dominating in the first half with a bold lead of 46-22 before the Serbs kicked into gear with a stunning comeback.

A quick 15-2 run trimmed the Aussie lead down to 11 points before the Boomers held off with a 12-points advantage at the end of the first half at 54-42.

But the second half was when the Serbians made their mark, edging ahead of the Aussies and forcing the team to catch up if they wanted to secure gold in Paris.

The heated match up came to a climax in the last seconds of regular play when Boomers captain Patty Mills forced the game into overtime after nailing a tough shot over Serbian star player Nikola Jokic, matching the scores at 82.

But the lead was sullied with Serbia putting themselves over the Aussies to cap the match off at 95-90.

Engrossed fans took to social media with impassioned commentary on the nail-biting game that had some devastated, while others were enraged and perplexed.

“The boomers choked on 24 point lead,” one wrote on X, formerly Twitter.

Another said: “The#Boomerswhat can I say. From elation to dismay, that game had everything.”

One penned: “Well that was a big load of stress before bed!”

One for the ages vs Serbia - but the Boomers campaign has come to an end.OT | AUS 90 - 95 SRBðŸ“Šhttps://t.co/bUGiddrYw8#WeAreBasketball|#GoBoomers|#AllezAUSpic.twitter.com/6zgjKtrdm6

A common theme amongst Aussie fans were complaints about the high turnover rate, with the Aussies throwing the ball away 20 times during the match.

“Tough way for the Boomers to bow out with turnovers the killer,” one fan wrote.

Another took aim at Aussie star player Josh Giddey, arguing the 21-year-old, “needed to be reminded that it is a team sport and not just him".

"So many turnovers, almost like an under 12 player,” they wrote.

One added: “Not good enough. Ordinary effort to lose in the end. Turnovers a killer.”

Giddey and Mills combined 51 points for the Boomers while Jokic led the Serbian side with 21 points.

Tuesday’s quarter-final loss marked the first time the Boomers were knocked out of the Olympics before the semi-finals since the London games in 2012.

The Boomers’ Olympics campaign has been mired with controversy over its contentious team selection.

Portland Trail Blazers winger Matisse Thybulle and Sydney Kings’ Xavier Cooks' omissions from the side were the topic of much contention.

Tuesday’s loss against Serbia finishes up the Boomers’ Olympics dreams, with just one win in four matches at the Paris games.

title: 'Choked': Fans devastated after Boomers blow 24-point lead against Serbia, get knocked out of 2024 Paris Olympic Games | Sky News Australia
description: Diehard fans have been devastated after the Australian men’s basketball team, the Boomers, crashed out of the Olympics after blowing its 24-point lead over Serbia.
keywords: 2024 Paris Olympic Games,Paddy Mills,Boomers vs Serbia,Josh Giddey,Olympic Games,Patty Mills,Boomers
